changes_made: Removed 'inventory' from resource names as it is not a valid resource type in Kubernetes.
Adjusted function names to better fit the naming pattern and resource types.
review_notes: The control CM-8-b is applicable to Kubernetes as maintaining an up-to-date inventory of system components can be mapped to ensuring proper labeling and annotation of resources like Pods and Nodes. However, the original function names did not align with the established naming conventions as 'inventory' is not a valid resource type. The changes made ensure that the function names are consistent with the pattern and accurately describe the checks being performed.
naming_improvements: Use specific resource names like 'pod' and 'node' instead of 'inventory' to align with Kubernetes resources.
title: System Component Inventory - Review and update the system...
step1_reasoning: The control CM-8-b focuses on maintaining an up-to-date inventory of system components. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that all resources such as Pods and Nodes are properly labeled and annotated for inventory purposes. Additionally, scheduling periodic reviews of these labels and annotations can help maintain an accurate inventory. This aligns with the need to have a systematic approach to tracking and managing Kubernetes resources.
